**First stable release.** Sync specific fields across machines —
not whole files.

## Highlights

- **Four format backends**: TOML, JSON / JSONC, gitconfig, and `.env`.
  Each preserves the target file's comments, blank lines, indentation,
  and quoting byte-stable. Built on `toml_edit`, `jsonc-parser`,
  `gix-config`, and a hand-rolled env CST.

- **Path syntax with array selectors**: pin an item by key
  (`mcp_servers[name="github"].enabled`), fan out across all items
  (`mcp_servers[name].enabled`), with strict typed literals (string /
  int / bool). Multi-match is treated as data corruption and bails
  before any write.

- **`dot-sync add`** registers new targets non-interactively, appends
  fields to an existing target, or opens an interactive ratatui tree
  picker that discovers fields directly from your source / target
  file.

- **Atomic writes + recovery snapshots.** Every write stages to a
  hidden tmp + renames; the previous contents land in
  `$TMPDIR/dot-sync/` so `dot-sync restore` can roll back any push
  in-the-moment. Persistent `<file>.bak.<timestamp>` is available via
  `--backup`. File permissions are preserved across the rename.

- **Conflict modes on `sync`**: `--target-wins` (default),
  `--source-wins`, or `--fail-on-conflict` (exit non-zero, write
  nothing). `pull` is always target-wins; `push` is always
  source-wins.

- **Format inference** from extension on `dot-sync add` (`.toml` /
  `.json` / `.jsonc` / `.gitconfig` / `.env` / `.envrc`; also the
  bare `.env` / `.envrc` dotfile names).

- **Shell completions** for bash / zsh / fish / PowerShell, plus a
  man page, generated on demand via `dot-sync completions <shell>`
  / `dot-sync man`. The installer's `--with-completions` flag wires
  the standard paths automatically.

## Install

```sh
curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/muhac/dot-sync/main/install.sh | sh
```

Pre-built binaries for **Linux x86_64**, **macOS arm64 / x86_64**,
and **Windows x86_64** are attached below.

## Coverage

414 tests across the four backends. Every supported feature and every
documented limitation lives in the README's per-format "specifics"
sections (collapsed under `<details>` blocks for quick reference).

## What's next

See the [README](https://github.com/muhac/dot-sync#readme) for usage
and the `dot-sync add` walkthrough. File issues at
<https://github.com/muhac/dot-sync/issues>.
